contacts.name: Marcus Webb
contacts.title: VP Engineering
contacts.confidence_reason: Exact economic buyer persona for a developer tooling platform. Hired 19 days ago from Stripe Platform team — actively in onboarding and environment assessment mode. Email pattern confirmed via company blog post byline.
contacts.notes: New VP Engineering (hired March 10, 2025) from Stripe's internal platform team. Prime evaluation window. Has 2,300 LinkedIn followers, active poster — his last post was about SRE culture at scale. Would respond well to a peer-level message from an engineering practitioner, not a sales pitch. Approach via LinkedIn message first before email.

contacts.name: Talia Osei
contacts.title: Head of Platform Engineering
contacts.confidence_reason: Platform lead = primary owner of on-call tooling, observability stack, and incident management decisions. Has been in role 14 months. Email pattern confirmed. LinkedIn shows she posted about their Kubernetes migration 3 months ago.
contacts.notes: Already in CRM from a prior outbound sequence 5 months ago — she replied once asking for a case study, then went quiet. The new VP Engineering hire and Series B are strong reasons to re-engage. Lead with 'A lot has changed since we last spoke' angle. She is the day-to-day champion and likely advises Marcus Webb on tooling.

contacts.name: Ravi Sundaram
contacts.title: Staff Engineer — Infrastructure
contacts.confidence_reason: Staff Engineer with infrastructure focus will be involved in any technical evaluation or proof of concept. Title is a technical evaluator, not a buyer — include only in later-stage sequences. LinkedIn active (post last month about OpenTelemetry).
contacts.notes: Long-tenured infrastructure engineer — knows the current stack deeply and will be a key voice in any PoC or integration review. No email confirmed. Do not sequence until Marcus Webb or Talia Osei are engaged and moving forward — Ravi is a stage 2 or 3 contact.

primary_contact.name: Marcus Webb
primary_contact.title: VP Engineering
primary_contact.rationale: 19 days into the role, active LinkedIn presence, direct economic buyer authority for developer tooling. The combination of a new VP Engineering hire + Series B + active SRE hiring creates a rare 60-day window where evaluation decisions are still being formed. Reaching him now before he inherits the incumbent vendor's renewal conversation is the highest-leverage move.

output.discovery_notes: No CTO found at DevCo — likely a flat structure where Marcus Webb (VP Engineering) holds both budget and technical leadership. Priya Nair (CEO) is listed on LinkedIn but approaching the CEO for a developer tooling decision at a 155-person company would be unusual and may signal a lack of understanding of their org. Stick with Marcus as primary. Talia Osei in CRM had prior warm engagement — coordinate re-engagement timing with the Marcus outreach to avoid confusion if both reply simultaneously.
